title: You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travel + Leisure ranked Boston third among U.S. cities for public transportation systems, behind Washington, D.C. and Champaign-Urbana.
The MBTA was praised for its extensive bus network, aiding car-free living by covering areas not accessible by train.
The MBTA’s Commuter Rail connects to destinations like Salem and Gillette Stadium, currently offering summer fare promotions.

title: Wu gets rid of green infrastructure office, lays off director
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu eliminates Boston's Office of Green Infrastructure; its functions move to the Streets Cabinet.
Director Max Rome is laid off; position removed in the fiscal year 2027 budget.
Green infrastructure planning to be fully integrated into citywide projects, affecting major streets initiatives.

title: Boston reports uptick in mpox cases
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Health Commission reports an increase in mpox cases and urges high-risk residents to get vaccinated.
Mpox vaccines will be offered during the Pride flag-raising at Boston City Hall on June 1, 1-4 p.m.
Globally, mpox cases increased by 10.2% in early 2026, with significant vaccination efforts ongoing in Boston.

title: Citgo sign goes dark — temporarily
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The landmark Citgo sign in Kenmore Square is being moved 30 feet higher and 120 feet east for visibility.
Citgo and Suffolk Construction began the relocation, with completion expected by December 2026, amid a two-phase process.
The sign, dark during relocation, has been a Boston skyline feature since 1940, despite past removal discussions.

title: Canton police respond to 2 ‘significant incidents’ at controversial therapy center
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton police responded to two significant incidents at the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center in late May.
The incidents sent a police officer and two residents to the hospital; one resident faces criminal charges.
The Judge Rotenberg Center is controversial for using electric shock therapy on patients with disabilities.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘unsettled weather’ into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Unsettled weather expected in Boston with showers starting Friday night and continuing into Saturday.
Saturday will see strong gusty winds, up to 50 mph along the coast, and unusually chilly temperatures.
Weather to improve briefly on Sunday, but intermittent showers will persist into mid-week before warming up later.

title: Former home-care aide charged in multimillion-dollar jewelry heist from JP home
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cory Kisakye, 27, a former home-care aide, was charged in a multimillion-dollar jewelry heist in Jamaica Plain.
Kisakye allegedly stole diamond rings, luxury watches, and gold jewelry from a safe, valued in millions, on Nov. 19, 2025.
Authorities linked Kisakye to the crime through surveillance footage, fingerprint evidence, and GPS data near the scene.

title: NBA insider’s take on the Celtics-Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brian Windhorst expressed skepticism about Celtics-Giannis trade rumors, citing no serious offers for Antetokounmpo.
Antetokounmpo, a two-time MVP, entering the final year of his contract with a player option for 2027-28.
Any trade involving Antetokounmpo would significantly bolster the Celtics' front court, aiming to improve playoff performance.

title: Human remains found in Worcester
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Worcester police discovered human remains near 4 Brandt Lane on Thursday afternoon.
The case has been accepted by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner for autopsy.
The Worcester District Attorney's office has been notified; detectives are investigating the incident.

title: Trooper in cruiser struck while working detail on I-93
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts state trooper hit in cruiser during I-93 detail in Quincy Thursday night.
The incident occurred at 10:35 p.m.; two lanes were closed due to roadwork at the time.
Trooper treated for minor injuries at Boston Medical Center; crash is under investigation.
